title: What is ELSS? Tax Saving Mutual Fund
meta_description: ELSS (Equity Linked Savings Scheme) saves tax under Section 80C with just 3-year lock-in. Learn how it works, returns, and best ELSS funds.

hero.one_liner: The only mutual fund that saves tax — invest up to ₹1.5 lakh under 80C with the shortest lock-in of just 3 years

sections.heading: What is ELSS?
content: ELSS stands for Equity Linked Savings Scheme. It's a type of mutual fund that invests primarily in stocks (minimum 80% in equity). The key feature — your investment qualifies for tax deduction under Section 80C up to ₹1.5 lakh per year, potentially saving you up to ₹46,800 in tax (at 30% slab + cess).

What makes ELSS special among 80C options is the shortest lock-in period — just 3 years. Compare this to PPF (15 years), NSC (5 years), or tax-saver FD (5 years). Plus, ELSS has historically delivered 12-15% returns, far more than other 80C options. The trade-off is that returns are market-linked and not guaranteed.

sections.heading: ELSS vs Other 80C Options
sections.footnote: ELSS has the highest return potential and shortest lock-in among all 80C options.

steps.title: Complete KYC on any mutual fund app
steps.description: Sign up on Groww, Zerodha Coin, or Kuvera. Complete KYC with PAN + Aadhaar (one-time, 5 minutes).

steps.title: Search for ELSS funds
steps.description: Filter by 'ELSS' or 'Tax Saving' category. Look for funds with consistent 5+ year track record. Popular choices: Mirae Asset Tax Saver, Axis Long Term Equity, SBI Long Term Equity.

steps.title: Start SIP or invest lump sum
steps.description: SIP of ₹12,500/month automatically invests ₹1.5 lakh by March (maxing out 80C). Or invest lump sum before March 31 deadline. Each SIP installment has its own 3-year lock-in.

steps.title: Hold beyond 3 years for tax efficiency
steps.description: After 3-year lock-in, you can sell. But holding longer gives better compounding. Gains above ₹1.25 lakh in a year are taxed at 12.5% (LTCG).

faqs.question: Is ELSS risky?
faqs.answer: ELSS invests in stocks, so short-term volatility is normal. You might see 10-20% drops in a bad year. However, with a 3-year lock-in, you're forced to stay invested through volatility — which historically has worked in investors' favor.

faqs.question: Can I invest more than ₹1.5 lakh in ELSS?
faqs.answer: Yes, but only ₹1.5 lakh gets the 80C tax deduction. Additional investment works like any regular equity mutual fund — no extra tax benefit.

faqs.question: ELSS SIP or lump sum — which is better?
faqs.answer: SIP is generally safer because it averages your cost over the year. But note: each SIP installment has its own 3-year lock-in. A January SIP unlocks in January 3 years later, a February SIP in February, and so on.

faqs.question: What happens after 3 years?
faqs.answer: Your units are automatically unlocked after 3 years. You can either sell them or continue holding. There's no pressure to sell — many people keep their ELSS investments running for 5-10 years for better returns.

disclaimer: This article is for educational purposes only. Mutual fund investments are subject to market risks. Past returns do not guarantee future performance. Consult a SEBI-registered advisor before investing.
